hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — 8, 10. You raised before the cards were dealt. Your opponent called.
Turn — 4. You bet again. Your opponent raised you.

wrongReasons: 9 9 has similar strength, but look at when the aggression shifted — the turn card connects with the correct hand in a way it doesn't connect with 9 9.
K 2 doesn't benefit from the turn card the way the correct hand does. A player holding only three of a kind wouldn't have the confidence to raise on that street.
5 8 doesn't benefit from the turn card the way the correct hand does. A player holding only straight wouldn't have the confidence to raise on that street.
hand.explanation:
Answer: 7 3

Your opponent flopped a full house and slow-played the flop, calling to disguise their strength. Once the pot was big enough they raised the turn and shoved the river — a classic trap with a monster hand.
